text.div: No narrative is provided for definitional resources. A human-readable rendering can be found in the implementation guide(s) where this resource is used.

name: MedMijCoreLmHealthProfessional
title: HealthProfessional
description: A health professional is a person who is authorized to perform actions in the field of individual healthcare.
purpose: This LogicalModel represents the HealthProfessional Clinical Information Model (CIM) for patient use cases in the context of MedMij. It is largely based on the Dutch [zib ('Zorginformatiebouwsteen', i.e. Health and Care Information Model) HealthProfessional v3.5 (2020)](https://zibs.nl/wiki/HealthProfessional-v3.5(2020EN)) adjusted such that it is suitable for implementation purposes in the context of MedMij.
copyright: Copyright and related rights waived via CC0, https://creativecommons.org/publicdomain/zero/1.0/. This does not apply to information from third parties, for example a medical terminology system. The implementer alone is responsible for identifying and obtaining any necessary licenses or authorizations to utilize third party IP in connection with the specification or otherwise.

element.definition: A health professional is a person who is authorized to perform actions in the field of individual healthcare.

The health professional identification number is a number that uniquely identifies the health professional.
The following numbers are used in the Netherlands: 1. UZI Health Professionals. Identification of health professionals (people) in the Dutch healthcare industry. 2. VEKTIS AGB-Z. Identifies health professionals and healthcare organizations 3. BIG-ID. The ID of the health professional listed in the BIG Register.
This information is not readily available for foreign health professionals.
Health professional's full name. If a health professional identification number is entered, it will be the name as listed in UZI, AGB or by the healthcare center.
The official first names of the health professional.
The initials of the health professional.
The name normally used to address the health professional.
This concept indicates the last name or order of last names with which the health professional is to be addressed.
Container of the LastName concept. This container contains all data elements of the LastName concept.
Prefix to the last name of the health professional.
The official last name of the health professional.
Container of the LastNamePartner concept. This container contains all data elements of the LastNamePartner concept.
Prefix to the partner's last name of the health professional.
The partner's official last name of the health professional.
Noble and scientific titles. These can assist in formulating oral and formal addresing titles.
Health professional's medical specialty. This refers to the recognized medical specialties as stated in the BIG Act. For example general practitioner or cardiologist.
Administrative gender of the health professional.
Health professional's address information.
Street name of the address.
House number of the address.
A letter following the house number as assigned by the municipal authorities.
The letters or signs needed to locate the mailbox, in addition to the house number and letter.
The indication used for addresses which do not consist of the usual street name and house number.

Postcode of the address.
In Dutch addresses, preferably use the postcode from the Postcode table (OID: 2.16.840.1.113883.2.4.4.15).

A geographically determined area which forms part of the municipal area.
For Dutch places of residence, preferably use the name from the GBA, table 33 (OID: 2.16.840.1.113883.2.4.6.14).

Municipality of residence.
For Dutch municipalities, preferably use the name from the GBA, table 33 (OID: 2.16.840.1.113883.2.4.6.14).
Country in which the address is located.
Extra information such as the building name, building number, entrance, route number.
The type of address in question, such as a home address or mailing address.
Health professional's telephone number(s) or e-mail address(es).
Container of the TelephoneNumbers concept. This container contains all data elements of the TelephoneNumbers concept.
The health professional's telephone number.
The telecom or device type that the telephone number is connected to.
The number type indicates whether the number is a landline, temporary or work telephone number.

Explanation about the telephone number and how to use it. It can be indicated, for example, that it is a department number (for healthcare professionnals) or that availability by phone is only possible during a specified part of the day.
Container of the EmailAddresses concept. This container contains all data elements of the E-mailAddresses concept.
The health professional's e-mail address.
The e-mail address type indicates whether it is a private or business e-mail address.
The organization the health professional works for.

The organization's identification number. For Dutch healthcare providers, the URA number or the AGB number is used, if possible. Depending on the context other IDs are also possible. For foreign or non-affiliated healthcare providers, another unique identification number can be used. This must be accompanied by the name and/or code of the issuing organization.
Name of the organization. If an identification number is given, the name must match the name that corresponds to the identification number.

The specialty of the healthcare provider's department. The departmental specialty can be filled in if further indication of the healthcare provider is needed. This refers to the recognized medical specialties as stated in the BIG Act.
The information needed to contact the healthcare organization via telephone and/or e-mail.
Container of the TelephoneNumbers concept. This container contains all data elements of the TelephoneNumbers concept.
The healthcare provider's telephone number.
The telecom or device type that the telephone number is connected to.
The number type indicates whether the number is a landline, temporary or work telephone number.

Explanation about the telephone number and how to use it. It can be indicated, for example, that it is a department number (for healthcare professionnals) or that availability by phone is only possible during a specified part of the day.
Container of the EmailAddresses concept. This container contains all data elements of the E-mailAddresses concept.
The healthcare provider's e-mail address.
The e-mail address type indicates whether it is a private or business e-mail address.
The physical address of the healthcare provider's location.
Street name of the address.
House number of the address.
A letter following the house number as assigned by the municipal authorities.
The letters or signs needed to locate the mailbox, in addition to the house number and letter.
The indication used for addresses which do not consist of the usual street name and house number.

Postcode of the address.
In Dutch addresses, preferably use the postcode from the Postcode table (OID: 2.16.840.1.113883.2.4.4.15).

A geographically determined area which forms part of the municipal area.
For Dutch places of residence, preferably use the name from the GBA, table 33 (OID: 2.16.840.1.113883.2.4.6.14).

Municipality of residence.
For Dutch municipalities, preferably use the name from the GBA, table 33 (OID: 2.16.840.1.113883.2.4.6.14).
Country in which the address is located.
Extra information such as the building name, building number, entrance, route number.
The type of address in question, such as a home address or mailing address.

The type of healthcare provider, such as general hospital, or nursing home. If this field is filled in and an AGB code is used for the HealthcareProviderIdentificationNumber, the type must match the type implicitly contained in the AGB code.
Container of the OrganizationLocation concept. This container contains all data elements of the OrganizationLocation concept.
Name of the location, in case a healthcare organization has more than one location.
Number of the location, if a numerical location identification is used next to or instead of a location name.
